key_insight: Danus scales mathematical proof search by coordinating multiple agents around a shared fact graph that stores verified claims, proofs, and dependencies.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical orchestration pattern for long-horizon AI reasoning systems that need parallel search, reliable memory, and auditable intermediate state.
method: Main agent plans, summarizes progress, and redirects search across workers.
Parallel worker agents explore proof ideas independently.
A stateless verifier admits only checked facts into the shared fact graph with dependencies.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports six research-level case studies across algebraic geometry, singularity theory, and combinatorics, showing the system can build long, detailed proofs.
watch_item: The approach depends on accurate verification and dependency tracking, so scalability and error propagation in the fact graph may become difficult in larger or noisier proof settings.
paper_title: Danus: Orchestrating Mathematical Reasoning Agents with Fact-Graph Memory
